act_break_transitions.current_transition: The last ember dies; darkness holds the glade. Cut to: first grey light on a Russian palisade wall
act_break_transitions.proposal: Pleiades burning cold above the pines — cut to first grey light on Russian palisade. Sky-to-sky visual match: Chechen night becomes Russian dawn. The irrevocable crossing encoded in one continuous visual arc. Current 'last ember dies — darkness holds — cut to first grey light' already approximates this; make the sky explicit in the shot description.

act_break_transitions.current_transition: The sound of Aksinya's wailing cuts abruptly — replaced by wind over open steppe, carrying into the next scene
act_break_transitions.proposal: Avdeev's wife going back to work — silent grinding erasure — CONTRAST CUT to Vorontsov's lamplit palace receiving a dust-caked courier as a prize. Maximum tonal opposition: domestic death vs. imperial spectacle. The empire that erased Avdeev is now receiving HM as a trophy.

motif_connections.visual_rhyme: Horse as agency and freedom (S002) → horse as desperate flight (S029) → horses shying at death (S052): HM’s power traced through the animal he controls
Lamp as safety (S001) → candle pinched out as certainty collapses (S015) → last ember as last life (S042): fire = civilization’s fragility
Hands on dagger (S006) → same hands bloodied in memory (S050): HM’s agency written in his own body across time
Gate closing = covenant sealed (S001) → door opening = surrender crossed (S029) → threshold = point of no return (S044)
Pleiades burning cold (S004) → Russian dawn (S015): same indifferent sky over both sides of the war
Muezzin (S001) → owl cry (S016) → river gurgle (S050): nature’s sounds mark the last free night in Chechen territory
